title: malaxis termensis var. elata
intro: Malaxis termensis var. elata is a rare and lesser-known plant species, also known as the Elate Spiranthes or Elate Ladies' Tresses, which belongs to the orchid family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Malaxis termensis var. elata include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
